name: Marketing Expressive

sans.name: Inter
sans.rationale: A neutral, highly legible workhorse for paragraphs and supporting text so the display face can carry personality without compromising readability.

mono.name: Space Mono
mono.role: Accents, labels, eyebrow text, code snippets
mono.rationale: A characterful monospace used sparingly for eyebrow labels, stats, and technical accents to add texture against the display and body faces.

display.name: Clash Display
display.rationale: A high-impact contemporary display face with strong personality for oversized marketing headlines; reserved for large sizes where its character reads well and never used for body copy.

scale.ratio: 1.333 (perfect fourth) for dramatic marketing hierarchy, with fluid clamp() scaling on hero sizes
scale.notes: Body stays at base/md for comfortable reading; the large end of the scale is reserved for the display face. Hero sizes use clamp() so headlines scale fluidly between mobile and desktop without breakpoints.

line_heights.tight: 1.05 (oversized hero display, where large type needs negative-ish leading)

fallback_stacks.sans: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
fallback_stacks.mono: 'Space Mono', ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace

localisation_notes: Display faces like Clash Display often have limited language coverage; provide a strong sans fallback and test non-Latin headlines, which may need to drop to the body sans
Subset and preload the display font (it is only used for a few large headlines) to protect LCP and avoid layout shift; use font-display: swap with a metrics-matched fallback
Reduce display line-height as size increases; the 1.05 tight value prevents oversized hero lines from feeling gappy
For pricing or stat blocks, opt into tabular figures locally even though body default is proportional
evidence: Pairing a characterful display face with a neutral body sans is a standard editorial/marketing practice for expressive yet readable pages
Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS) make font subsetting, preloading, and metrics-matched fallbacks a measurable requirement for hero typography
WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.8 line-spacing guidance still applies to body copy regardless of expressive display sizing
